title: indigofera aspera
intro: Indigofera aspera, also known as Prickly Indigo or Rough Indigo, is a flowering plant species in the pea family, characterized by its thorny stems and clusters of small white flowers. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Indigofera aspera include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Indigofera aspera can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to make a paste or powder for topical application.
